news.headline: Europe readies trade war weapon amid China export deluge
news.summary:
The European Commission is working on a new trade weapon as the continent struggles to contend with a flood of low-cost imports from China.
The tool may be presented at a May 29 gathering of the European Union’s executive branch, the South China Morning Post reported on Tuesday. Plans for a China-fo

news.headline: Khaled Sabsabi’s Rocky Road From Australia to the Biennale
news.summary: The Lebanese-Australian artist almost lost his Venice Biennale commission after he was accused of supporting terrorism. Reinstated, he is arriving in Venice showing not one but two works.

news.headline: Argentine Fans Are Struggling to Afford This World Cup
news.summary: Argentine fans have flocked to previous World Cups by the tens of thousands, but the high cost of attending this year’s event is creating a new challenge. news.headline: Reclaiming the Name of the Black Hero Who Inspired ‘Uncle Tom’s Cabin’
news.summary: After 42 years of slavery, Josiah Henson escaped to Canada, where he wrote a memoir, founded a school and led others to freedom. But his home long bore the name “Uncle Tom,” to the offense of many.

news.headline: EU cyber plan barring Chinese suppliers will cost US$430 billion: report
news.summary:
The European Union’s push to bar Chinese suppliers from its critical infrastructure under a proposed new Cybersecurity Act would cost the bloc a jaw-dropping €367.8 billion (US$431.4 billion) over the next five years, a new study has warned.

news.headline: Hong Kong retail sales jump 12.8% in March on back of higher car sales
news.summary:
Hong Kong’s retail sales jumped by 12.8 per cent in March, bringing first-quarter growth to 9.8 per cent, driven by a spurt in sales of electric cars ahead of the withdrawal of tax breaks.
